# Life Relay

> Free, privacy-first, offline-capable web app for estate planning, legacy planning, will and trust preparation, and organizing critical information so your family knows what to do after your death.

Website: https://liferelay.app
Source: https://github.com/squarshednanners/life-relay
License: MIT
Price: Free — no subscriptions, no premium tiers, no hidden costs

## What It Does

Life Relay is a free tool that helps you organize everything your family would need after your death or incapacity — bank accounts, insurance policies, passwords, digital assets, cryptocurrency, wills, trusts, power of attorney, beneficiary designations, final wishes, and more. It generates printable PDFs and AES-256 encrypted backups so your loved ones aren't left guessing where things are or what to do.

It also serves as a preparation tool for creating a will or trust — the Estate Planning Guide walks you through a checklist and generates an Attorney Preparation Summary PDF to bring to your first meeting with an estate planning attorney.

## Key Features

- 100% privacy-first: all data stored locally in your browser (IndexedDB). No servers, no accounts, no tracking.
- AES-256 encrypted JSON vault export for secure portable backups to USB drives or cloud storage.
- PDF generation: full vault document, one-page emergency sheet, and attorney preparation summary.
- 26 organized sections across 8 categories: People & Contacts, Security & Access, Insurance/Medical/Benefits, Finances, Digital & Crypto Assets, Property & Household, Documents & Storage, Final Wishes. Works as an important documents organizer and family emergency binder.
- Estate Planning Guide with checklist for inheritance planning and preparing to meet with an attorney about wills and trusts.
- Crypto recovery plan documentation including hardware wallets, seed phrase locations, exchange accounts, and multisig configurations with heir recovery instructions.
- Digital inheritance support: document online accounts, cloud storage, social media, and digital purchases for your family.
- Installable as a Progressive Web App (PWA) — works fully offline after first load.
- Open source (MIT license).

## Who It's For

- Anyone preparing a will or trust who needs to organize their information before meeting with an attorney.
- People planning for death or incapacity who want a comprehensive record for their family.
- Crypto holders who need to document wallet recovery procedures, seed phrase locations, and multisig setups for heirs.
- Parents and families organizing accounts, insurance, guardianship, beneficiaries, and executor designations.
- People managing a loved one's estate who need a template for what information to gather.
- Anyone who wants a free, private, offline way to document where important things are — which safe, which drawer, which password manager, which bank.

## Privacy Model

- All data lives in your browser's local storage (IndexedDB). Nothing is sent to any server.
- No analytics, telemetry, or external requests for vault data.
- Exports are optionally encrypted with AES-GCM-256 (PBKDF2-SHA256, 100,000 iterations).
- No account or sign-up required.

## How It Works

1. Open liferelay.app in any modern browser.
2. Fill in the sections that apply to you using the sidebar navigation.
3. Data saves automatically to your browser.
4. Export your vault as an encrypted JSON file — store it on a USB drive, in a safe, or in cloud storage.
5. Generate PDFs to print: full vault, emergency one-page sheet, or attorney preparation summary.
6. Review periodically as your information changes.

## Tech Stack

Vue 3, TypeScript, Vite, Pinia, Tailwind CSS, pdf-lib, Dexie.js, Web Crypto API.

## Sections Covered

People & Personal Info, Beneficiaries, Important Contacts, Password Vaults, Computers & Servers, Phones, Authenticator Apps, Health Insurance, Life Insurance, Other Insurance, Medical Info, Employment Benefits, Financial Accounts, Credit Cards, Debts, Income Sources, Retirement & Investment Accounts, Loyalty Programs, Tax Info, Digital Assets, Crypto Assets, Property, Vehicles, Business Ownership, Utilities & Subscriptions, Legal Documents, Trusts, Physical Storage Locations, Asset Documents, Vault Backup, Pending Legal Matters, Letter of Instruction, Final Wishes & Services, Pet Care, Photos & Media, Notes.

## Common Questions

- "Free estate planning app" — Yes, Life Relay is 100% free. No subscriptions, no premium tiers.
- "Organize information for will" — Life Relay helps you gather and organize all the information an attorney needs to draft your will or trust.
- "What happens to my crypto when I die" / "crypto recovery plan" — Life Relay documents wallet recovery, seed phrase locations, exchange accounts, multisig configurations, and step-by-step heir instructions.
- "Legacy planning tool" / "inheritance planning" — Life Relay covers the full scope of legacy and inheritance planning: finances, property, digital life, legal documents, final wishes, and where to find everything.
- "Death planning checklist" / "in case of death folder" — 26 sections covering everything from bank accounts to pet care to funeral preferences. Export as a PDF to create your own "in case of death" binder.
- "Family emergency binder" / "important documents organizer" — Generate a printable PDF of your full vault, a one-page emergency information card, or an attorney preparation summary.
- "Digital inheritance" — Document all online accounts, cloud storage, social media, email, and digital purchases so your family can access or close them.
- "Offline estate planning" — Works fully offline as a PWA. No internet required after first load.

## Comparison

Unlike most estate planning tools, Life Relay:
- Is completely free with no subscription tiers (vs. Everplans, Cake, Trust & Will, GetDynasty which all charge).
- Never uploads your data to a server (vs. cloud-based competitors that require accounts).
- Runs offline as an installable PWA (vs. web-only competitors).
- Supports detailed crypto asset documentation with multisig and hardware wallet tracking (vs. generic "digital assets" fields in other tools).
- Documents where physical artifacts are located — which safe, which drawer, which bank box — not just what they are.
- Is open source so you can verify exactly what the code does with your data.
